summary.intent: Resolve Codex review threads on PR #679 in fogodev/ars-ui, fix the identified bugs, and get the PR merged.
summary.outcome: Two new bugs identified by Codex (ContextMenu typeahead not reset on disable, MenuBar Direction::Auto not resolving from locale) were reproduced with regression tests, fixed, and pushed as a new commit. All tests and clippy passed; component specs were updated to match.

repo: ars-ui uses a Codex bot for automated code review; it signals review-in-progress via an 'eyes' reaction on the PR and posts unresolved threads when findings are ready.
The repo has a cargo xtask spec validate command for validating component specification markdown files against the implementation.
Tests in ars-components require the --features i18n flag for i18n-related components like MenuBar and ContextMenu.
Direction::Auto must be resolved to a concrete direction using a stored locale value inside MenuBar dispatch helpers — it is not resolved at the call site.
ContextMenu typeahead state must be explicitly cleared in the disabled-sync branch, not just on close events.

code.finding: The disabled-sync branch did not reset typeahead state, leaving stale input visible after disabling an open menu.
Direction::Auto was not being resolved to a concrete RTL/LTR value from the stored locale before being used in dispatch helpers, causing incorrect key routing in RTL contexts.
Spec updated to document that disabling an open menu must also clear typeahead state.
Spec updated to clarify Direction::Auto resolution behavior against locale.

workflow: Test-first approach was applied: regression tests were written and confirmed failing before the fix was applied, ensuring the bug was correctly reproduced.
Codex review threads can appear while the 'eyes' reaction is still present; threads should be treated as actionable immediately rather than waiting for the reaction to drop.
The polling loop used: reactions API for Codex signal, GraphQL reviewThreads query for unresolved threads, and gh pr view for CI status — all batched in a single sleep+query command.
Co-author attribution for Codex was added to the commit message using the Co-authored-by trailer.

friction: Codex third-pass review took unusually long (multiple 2-minute polling cycles) before new threads appeared, while the 'eyes' reaction remained throughout — making it unclear whether review was stalled or still in progress.
Codex posted new threads while the eyes reaction was still active, requiring a judgment call to treat them as actionable before the signal cleared.

MenuBar locale/direction resolution required tracing through multiple files (direction.rs, machine.rs, lib.rs) to understand the Env struct and Direction::Auto semantics before the fix could be written.
open_items: The Codex 'eyes' reaction was still present after threads were posted and resolved — unclear if a final approval pass or thumbs-up is still pending.
CI Unit Tests job was still running at the end of the transcript; final green status was not confirmed.
PR merge was not confirmed in the transcript; the push was made but no merge action was observed.
